To find the values of $ a $ and $ b $ that satisfy the condition that the surface area of the shape is exactly 1000 square units we begin by analyzing the given parametric equations:

$$
x(u, v) = a \sin(u) \cos(v)
$$
$$
y(u, v) = a \sin(u) \sin(v)
$$
$$
z(u, v) = b \cos(u)
$$

This parametric form describes a surface of revolution known as a **prolate spheroid** if $ a \neq b $ or a **sphere** if $ a = b $. The surface area of a surface given by parametric equations $ \vec{r}(u, v) = (x(u, v), y(u, v), z(u, v)) $ can be calculated using the formula:

$$
A = \iint_{D} \left\| \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} \right\| \, du \, dv
$$

First compute the partial derivatives:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} = \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v), a \cos(u) \sin(v), -b \sin(u) \right)
$$
$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = \left( -a \sin(u) \sin(v), a \sin(u) \cos(v), 0 \right)
$$

Now compute the cross product $ \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} $:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = 
\begin{vmatrix}
\mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\
a \cos(u) \cos(v) & a \cos(u) \sin(v) & -b \sin(u) \\
-a \sin(u) \sin(v) & a \sin(u) \cos(v) & 0
\end{vmatrix}
$$
